questions.assertion: Assertion (A): When a wave is reflected from a fixed end, the displacement of the particle at the end is zero.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is because the reflected wave is inverted with respect to the incident wave.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: When a wave is reflected from a fixed end, the displacement of the particle at the end is indeed zero. This is because the reflected wave is inverted with respect to the incident wave, effectively canceling out the displacement at the fixed end.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Superposition of two waves results in a wave of greater amplitude.
questions.reason: Reason (R): When two waves superpose, the particles of the two waves displace each other in the same direction.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: When two waves superpose, the resultant amplitude can indeed be greater than the individual amplitudes of the waves. This is because the particles of the two waves displace each other in the same direction, resulting in a greater displacement and hence a greater amplitude.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A standing wave has a node at its fixed end.
questions.reason: Reason (R): A node is a point of zero displacement in a standing wave.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: A standing wave indeed has a node at its fixed end, which is a point of zero displacement. This is because the fixed end does not allow any displacement of particles, resulting in a node.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The equation of a progressive wave is y = a sin (kx - ωt),
questions.reason: Reason (R): The equation can be written in the form y = a sin (kx - ωt) by using the general form of a wave.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The equation of a progressive wave can indeed be written in the form y = a sin (kx - ωt), which is a general form of a wave. This equation represents a wave propagating in the x-direction with a frequency ω and wave number k.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): When two waves of same frequency and amplitude superpose, the resulting wave has double the amplitude.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The superposition of two waves results in a resultant wave that has an amplitude equal to the sum of the individual amplitudes.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: When two waves of the same frequency and amplitude superpose, the resulting wave indeed has double the amplitude. This is because the superposition of the two waves results in a resultant wave that has an amplitude equal to the sum of the individual amplitudes.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A standing wave has a crest at its free end.
questions.reason: Reason (R): A crest is a point of maximum displacement in a standing wave.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: A standing wave does not have a crest at its free end. Instead, it has an antinode at its free end, which is a point of maximum displacement.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The speed of a wave in a string is independent of its frequency.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The speed of a wave in a string depends only on the tension and mass per unit length of the string.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The speed of a wave in a string indeed does not depend on its frequency. The speed of a wave in a string depends only on the tension and mass per unit length of the string, as given by the equation v = √(T/μ), where T is the tension and μ is the mass per unit length.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The equation of a wave is y = a sin (ωt - kx),
questions.reason: Reason (R): The equation can be written in the form y = a sin (ωt - kx) by using the general form of a wave.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The equation of a wave can indeed be written in the form y = a sin (ωt - kx), which is a general form of a wave. This equation represents a wave propagating in the x-direction with a frequency ω and wave number k.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): When two waves of same frequency and amplitude superpose, the resulting wave has an amplitude equal to the individual amplitudes.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The superposition of two waves results in a resultant wave that has an amplitude that is the vector sum of the individual amplitudes.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: When two waves of the same frequency and amplitude superpose, the resulting wave does not have an amplitude equal to the individual amplitudes. Instead, the amplitude of the resulting wave is the vector sum of the individual amplitudes.
